EA030 — Environmental Analysis

Project Index

Prof. Marc Los Huertos  •  Pomona College

← Back to RTricks

This page indexes EA030 Environmental Analysis projects for the current semester. Each section will link to student work, datasets, reports, and interactive content as those become available. Check back as the term progresses.

Welcome

Regional Climate Trends

A project exploring regional temperature, precipitation, and extreme weather patterns using publicly available datasets (NOAA, PRISM, etc.).

Coming soon

Pitzer / pHake Lake Stratification

Seasonal stratification patterns, temperature profiles, and dissolved oxygen dynamics at pHake Lake on the Pitzer College campus.

Coming soon

Compost Maturity Analysis

Evaluation of compost temperature, moisture, C:N ratio, and stability indicators to assess maturity and readiness for field application.

Coming soon

Inland Empire Air Quality

EPA monitoring and PurpleAir sensor data used to analyze PM2.5 patterns across the Inland Empire, including diurnal cycles, seasonal variability, and comparisons with federal health standards.

See also: Analyzing EPA PM2.5 Data

Available

Surface Water Quality

Field sampling and lab-based analysis of nutrients, conductivity, turbidity, and other indicators across local surface water bodies.

Coming soon

Notes for Students

As each project develops, please submit:

  • Final HTML report (knitted from your .Rmd)
  • Key figures and data visualizations
  • Analytical scripts
  • A brief reflection or summary of findings

Links will be added here as work becomes available. Contact Prof. Los Huertos or drop files in the shared course folder.
